title: India Weather Turns Unpredictable: Heatwave And Storm Alerts Issued For June 11 - Dailyhunt
full_text:
India's weather on June 11, 2026, is showing two completely different faces at the same time. While several states, including Uttar Pradesh, Bihar, and Rajasthan, continue to battle intense heatwave conditions, thunderstorms, hailstorms, and monsoon rains are beginning to change the weather pattern.
The Southwest Monsoon is advancing steadily, while a strong Western Disturbance is creating unstable conditions across North India. People should stay prepared for sudden changes, as many areas may shift from scorching heat to strong storms within a few hours.
A Major Weather Shift Begins
June 11 marks an important turning point in this year's summer season. For weeks, many parts of India experienced extremely high temperatures. Now, weather systems from both the north and south are beginning to challenge that heat.
The Southwest Monsoon has already spread across large parts of South India. States like Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, and the entire Northeast region are receiving rainfall activity. At the same time, a Western Disturbance is entering Northwest India, bringing clouds, thunderstorms, dust storms, and chances of rain.
This combination is creating a dramatic weather transition across the country.
Uttar Pradesh Faces Double Trouble
Uttar Pradesh remains one of the most weather-sensitive states on June 11. The India Meteorological Department has issued warnings related to both heatwave conditions and severe thunderstorms.
Many districts may experience uncomfortable daytime temperatures. However, increasing moisture in the atmosphere is also raising the possibility of sudden storms.
Residents are advised not to ignore weather warnings. Conditions may change quickly during the afternoon and evening hours.
Eastern UP Under Alert
Eastern Uttar Pradesh is expected to witness heatwave conditions in isolated areas. Humidity levels may also rise, making the weather feel even more uncomfortable.
Meteorologists have warned of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and strong winds reaching speeds of 50 to 60 kmph. In some places, gusts may touch 70 kmph.
Districts located near the Bihar border could experience the most unstable weather conditions during the day.
Western UP Braces For Storm Activity
Western Uttar Pradesh is likely to see a mix of intense heat and severe weather activity.
Dust storms, hailstorms, and thunderstorms are possible due to the influence of the Western Disturbance affecting nearby regions. Light to moderate rainfall may also occur in several districts.
This weather pattern could provide relief from the heat, though strong winds may cause temporary disruptions in some areas.
Lucknow Weather Forecast
Lucknow is expected to witness a noticeable drop in temperature compared to earlier days of the month.
Daytime temperatures may remain between 34°C and 40°C. The sky is likely to remain partly cloudy before turning more overcast later in the day.
Rainfall and thunderstorms are possible during the evening or night hours. Moderate winds may also provide some relief from the heat.
Delhi, Haryana And Punjab On Watch
Northwest India remains under close observation.
Delhi, Haryana, Punjab, and Chandigarh are likely to experience thunderstorms, dust storms, and gusty winds. Some areas may also receive hailstorms.
While heatwave conditions continue in isolated locations, weather experts believe temperatures may gradually decrease as storm activity increases.
People travelling outdoors should remain alert, especially during evening hours.
Rajasthan Battles Heat And Storms
Rajasthan is facing a challenging weather situation.
Western Rajasthan remains under heatwave warnings. Meanwhile, eastern districts may experience thunderstorms and strong winds.
Some locations could witness wind speeds touching 70 kmph. Sudden weather changes may bring temporary relief from extreme temperatures.
Heavy Rain Alert For Himalayan States
The Western Disturbance is most active over the Himalayan region.
Uttarakhand is expected to receive heavy rainfall along with thunderstorms and hailstorm activity. Similar conditions are possible in parts of Himachal Pradesh and Jammu and Kashmir.
Strong winds and heavy showers may affect travel and outdoor activities in hilly regions.
Bihar And Jharkhand Continue To Sizzle
Bihar and Jharkhand remain among the hottest regions in the country.
Heatwave conditions are still expected in several districts. However, weather models indicate that temperatures may begin falling gradually from June 11 onward.
Isolated rainfall and thunderstorm activity could bring some welcome relief after days of extreme heat.
Monsoon Active Across South India
The monsoon is performing strongly across South India.
Kerala and Coastal Karnataka are expected to receive heavy rainfall. Some districts may remain under weather alerts due to intense rain activity.
Telangana and Andhra Pradesh are also witnessing growing monsoon influence. Scattered rainfall and thunderstorms are likely throughout the region.
Tamil Nadu may experience isolated heavy rainfall, especially in interior and hilly areas.
Northeast India Sees Widespread Rain
The Northeast continues to remain firmly under the grip of the monsoon.
Assam, Meghalaya, Arunachal Pradesh, and neighbouring states are likely to receive widespread rainfall. Heavy to very heavy showers are possible in several districts.
Low-lying areas may experience temporary waterlogging due to continuous rainfall.
What People Should Expect Today
June 11 is shaping up to be one of the most dynamic weather days of the month. Heatwave conditions continue in many northern and eastern states, but thunderstorms, rain, and strong winds are beginning to break the long spell of extreme summer heat.
For Uttar Pradesh especially, this day represents a weather crossover. Residents may wake up to intense heat but end the day under dark clouds, gusty winds, and possible rain.
Weather experts advise people to stay hydrated, avoid unnecessary exposure to afternoon heat, and remain updated with local forecasts, as conditions may change rapidly across large parts of India.
